turns.title: cli session sess-dem
turns.prompt: Refactor the parser to stream instead of buffering the whole file.
turns.reply: Done. I switched `parse()` to a generator that yields records line-by-line, so memory stays flat on large inputs. Tests pass.

turns.title: cli session sess-dem
turns.reply: Added `--limit N`. It short-circuits the generator after N records. Help text updated.

turns.title: On compression and meaning
turns.prompt: Is understanding the same as compression?
turns.thinking: The user is asking a real philosophy-of-mind question. I should avoid the glib 'yes, MDL!' answer and name the tension: lossless vs lossy, and that some truths may not compress. Give a crisp distinction, not a lecture.
turns.reply: Not quite. Compression is for storage; understanding is for use. A summary compresses; a theory compresses AND lets you generate cases you never stored. When a thing 'understands' it has found a generating function, not just a shorter list — and some truths may be irreducibly long.

turns.title: On compression and meaning
turns.prompt: so a proverb is compression that still works out of context?
turns.thinking: Good follow-up. The distinguishing property is transfer: a proverb holds in situations unlike the one that generated it. That's 'valid compression'. Keep it to two sentences.
turns.reply: Exactly — that's valid compression. A fact is true in one place; a proverb is true in every place that shares a certain shape. The eggs in 'don't put all your eggs in one basket' are a handle on variance under uncertainty, not a claim about eggs.

turns.title: community: alex
turns.reply: Happy to. Tell me what it does in one sentence and what feeling you want the name to carry, and I'll riff a dozen directions.
